modules.title: Terminal Navigation Basics
modules.purpose: Learn what the terminal is, how to know where you are, how to list files, and how to move between folders.
learningObjectives: Explain what a terminal command does.
Use pwd and ls to inspect the current location.
Use cd, ., .., and ~ to move around safely.
Create a simple folder map using terminal commands.
guidedExercises: Use terminal commands to change the workspace file tree
Verify progress with pwd and ls
Use terminal output checks for inspection commands and workspace checks for file-changing tasks
modules.moduleProject: Build a simple terminal map that proves you can create and navigate a nested workspace.

sections.title: Terminal Orientation
sections.summary: Start with the terminal prompt, current directory, listing files, and moving between folders.

topics.title: What the Terminal Is
topics.summary: Open with a standalone course welcome, then understand the terminal as a command conversation with a prompt, commands, output, and safe inspection habits.
learningGoals: Understand who this course is for, what the safe terminal workspace contains, and how the three modules build on one another.
Explain the prompt, command, and output parts of a terminal exchange.
Use inspect first, change second, verify last as the course safety habit.

topics.title: Where Am I? pwd and ls
topics.summary: Use pwd and ls to inspect your current folder and see what is inside it.
learningGoals: Use pwd and ls to inspect your current folder and see what is inside it.

topics.title: Moving Around with cd
topics.summary: Use cd, .., ., and ~ to move between folders safely.
learningGoals: Use cd, .., ., and ~ to move between folders safely.

sections.title: Module Project
sections.summary: Build a simple terminal map that proves you can create and navigate a nested workspace.

topics.title: Project: Terminal Map
topics.summary: Build a small folder map using pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, and touch.
learningGoals: Build a small folder map using pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, and touch.

modules.title: Files, Folders, and Viewing Content
modules.purpose: Create, copy, move, rename, delete, and inspect files from the terminal.
learningObjectives: Create folders and files with mkdir, mkdir -p, and touch.
Copy, move, and rename files using cp and mv.
Use safe deletion habits with rm.
Inspect text files with cat, head, tail, and wc.
guidedExercises: Use terminal commands to change the workspace file tree
Verify progress with pwd and ls
Use terminal output checks for viewing commands and workspace checks for file-changing tasks
modules.moduleProject: Organize a messy notes folder into a clean school workspace.

sections.title: File Workflow
sections.summary: Practice the commands developers use every day to build and organize project folders.

topics.title: Creating Folders and Files
topics.summary: Use mkdir, mkdir -p, and touch to create a useful project structure.
learningGoals: Use mkdir, mkdir -p, and touch to create a useful project structure.

topics.title: Copy, Move, and Rename
topics.summary: Use cp and mv to duplicate, organize, and rename files safely.
learningGoals: Use cp and mv to duplicate, organize, and rename files safely.

topics.title: Viewing File Contents
topics.summary: Inspect text files with cat, head, tail, and wc.
learningGoals: Inspect text files with cat, head, tail, and wc.

sections.title: Module Project
sections.summary: Organize a messy notes folder into a clean school workspace.

topics.title: Project: Notes Organizer
topics.summary: Organize a messy folder into notes, homework, code, and backups.
learningGoals: Organize a messy folder into notes, homework, code, and backups.

modules.title: Final Capstone: Community Media Archive
modules.purpose: Prove that inspection, navigation, creation, writing, reading, copying, moving, renaming, safe deletion, and verification can be combined without step-by-step command coaching.
learningObjectives: Inspect a workspace before changing it.
Create a clean folder structure for a real task.
Move, copy, rename, and remove files safely.
Verify the finished workspace with terminal commands.
guidedExercises: Inspect a messy workspace before organizing it
Create folders and marker files for a handoff
Use workspace validation to confirm the final structure
modules.moduleProject: Prepare and verify a Community Media Archive through five cumulative terminal-workspace milestones.

sections.title: Final Capstone
sections.summary: Organize and verify a community media archive in one cumulative project topic.

topics.title: Community Media Archive
topics.summary: Build, organize, protect, clean, and verify a media archive with the complete beginner terminal toolkit.
learningGoals: Inspect and navigate before making workspace changes.
Create nested folders and placeholder files with mkdir, mkdir -p, and touch.
Write and append guide text with echo, >, and >>, then inspect it with cat.
Move, rename, and copy important records with mv and cp.
Remove only the named temporary file with safe rm.
Verify the final structure with pwd, targeted ls, cat, and wc -l before marking it ready.

projectBrief.scenario: A neighborhood media team inherited a small photo-project archive with an inbox containing a photo index, release forms, shoot notes, and one temporary import file. The next volunteer needs a verified structure that explains where every record belongs.
projectBrief.role: Terminal workspace coordinator preparing a community media archive for the next volunteer
projectBrief.workspace: One cumulative terminal-only workspace rooted at media-archive/ with inbox/, organized/, backup/, and review/ folders
projectBrief.deliverable: One five-step Community Media Archive capstone that inspects the workspace, creates the structure, writes the guide, moves and renames records, backs up the photo index, removes only the temporary import, verifies the result, and creates organized/READY.txt.
requirements: Keep the capstone in exactly one capstone section and one capstone topic.
Carry the full working workspace from one step into the next.
Use only commands taught in this course: pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, touch, echo, redirection, cat, head, tail, wc -l, cp, mv, and safe single-file rm.
Require inspection before changes and a targeted verification pass after changes.
Forbid sudo, recursive rm, chmod, chown, package installation, external networking, and commands not taught in the course.
Keep every workspace path and expected result explicit and deterministic.

stepLadder.title: Inspect and Build the Archive Structure
stepLadder.requirement: Inspect media-archive, enter it, create organized/photos, organized/documents, backup, and review, then create review/needs-review.txt.

stepLadder.title: Write the Archive Guide and Review Note
stepLadder.requirement: Use echo with > and >> to write organized/README.txt and add the review instruction to review/needs-review.txt, then verify both with cat.

stepLadder.title: Move and Rename the Incoming Records
stepLadder.requirement: Move the photo index, release forms, and shoot notes into their final folders, renaming photo-index.txt to organized/photos/index.txt.

stepLadder.title: Back Up the Index and Remove the Temporary Import
stepLadder.requirement: Copy organized/photos/index.txt to backup/photo-index-backup.txt, remove only inbox/tmp-import.txt, and verify both results.

stepLadder.title: Verify the Archive and Mark It Ready
stepLadder.requirement: Use pwd, targeted ls, cat, and wc -l to verify the complete archive, then write and display organized/READY.txt.
